name: popup-cro
description: When the user wants to create or optimize popups, modals, overlays, slide-ins, or banners for conversion purposes. Also use when the user mentions "exit intent," "popup conversions," "modal optimization," "lead capture popup," "email popup," "announcement banner," or "overlay." For forms outside of popups, see form-cro. For general page conversion optimization, see page-cro.
capabilities: Design popup strategies based on purpose (email capture, lead magnet, discount, exit intent)
Recommend optimal triggers (time-based, scroll-based, exit intent, behavior-based)
Create conversion-focused copy for headlines, subheadlines, CTAs, and decline options
Advise on frequency capping and user preference storage to respect visitor choices
Ensure GDPR compliance and accessibility standards in popup implementation
A/B test hypotheses for popup variations and optimization strategies
author: coreyhaines31
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. This is a text-based conversion optimization skill containing only documentation about popup strategies. The scanner flagged generic text patterns as security issues (e.g., 'localStorage' references at line 230 for frequency capping, which is standard UX practice). No malicious code, cryptographic operations, or reconnaissance patterns exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Users struggle with intrusive popups that annoy visitors and hurt conversions. This skill provides expert strategies for creating popups, modals, and banners that convert without damaging user experience.
limitations: Does not generate actual code or HTML/CSS for popup implementation
Does not integrate with specific popup tools or platforms
Does not access real-time analytics or conversion data from your site
Does not provide legal compliance verification for specific jurisdictions

useCases.title: E-commerce Lead Capture
useCases.description: Design exit intent popups that capture email addresses from abandoning visitors with compelling discount offers.

useCases.title: SaaS Product Demos
useCases.description: Create click-triggered modals for demo requests and lead magnets that activate when users show interest.

useCases.title: Content Newsletter Growth
useCases.description: Build scroll-triggered popup strategies to convert engaged blog readers into newsletter subscribers.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Email Popup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an email capture popup for my [type of site]. My goal is to [primary conversion goal]. My target audience is [visitor description]. Please recommend the popup type, trigger timing, and provide sample headline, subheadline, and CTA button text.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a simple popup from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Exit Intent Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an exit intent popup strategy for my [e-commerce/SaaS/content] site. I want to capture [what] before visitors leave. Include the popup copy, trigger logic, and how this differs from my entry popup.
promptTemplates.scenario: Recovering abandoning visitors with exit intent

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Popup Funnel
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a multi-popup strategy for my site with [traffic description]. I want [number] popups that work together without annoying visitors. Include purpose, trigger, audience targeting, and conflict rules for each popup.

promptTemplates.title: Popup A/B Test Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an A/B test plan for my popup. I want to test [variable: headline/CTA/timing/design]. My current conversion rate is [X]%. Please provide test hypotheses with expected outcomes and recommended sample sizes.

bestPractices: Match popup offer to visitor intent and page context rather than using generic messaging.
Always provide clear value upfront and respect visitor choices with easy dismissal options.
Test one variable at a time (headline, timing, design) to understand what drives conversions.
antiPatterns: Using manipulative tactics like pre-checked opt-ins or misleading decline button labels.
Showing popups immediately on page load or before users can engage with content.
Ignoring mobile experience with aggressive full-screen overlays that block navigation.

faq.question: What is the best trigger for my popup?
faq.answer: The best trigger depends on your goal. Click-triggered works best for lead magnets (user-initiated, high intent). Scroll-based suits content engagement. Exit intent captures abandoning visitors. Time-delays (30+ seconds) work for general awareness but risk annoying users.

faq.question: How often should I show popups to the same visitor?
faq.answer: Show at most once per session. Use cookies or localStorage to remember dismissals for 7-30 days. Respect when users say no—they have already made a choice.

faq.question: Do popups hurt SEO?
faq.answer: Google penalizes intrusive interstitials that block content on mobile. Cookie notices, age verification, and reasonable banners are fine. Avoid full-screen popups that appear before content on mobile devices.

faq.question: What conversion rate should I expect?
faq.answer: Typical benchmarks: Email popups 2-5%, Exit intent 3-10%, Click-triggered 10%+ (self-selected audience). Your results depend on offer relevance, timing, and traffic quality.

faq.question: How do I make popups accessible?
faq.answer: Ensure keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Esc to close). Use focus trapping while modal is open. Add ARIA labels for screen readers. Maintain color contrast ratios. Never rely on color alone to convey information.

faq.question: Can I use the same popup on mobile and desktop?
faq.answer: Not recommended. Mobile users have different behaviors and screen constraints. Use bottom slide-ups or full-width banners on mobile instead of center modals. Exit intent cannot be detected on mobile—use scroll depth or time-based triggers instead.
